pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in printed postcard/leaflet from Cremona showing five famous historic violins (Amati, Guarneri, Stradivari). No handwritten text on this page; no handwritten page number visible in the top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
We left & passed through a spa town, __Salso[?Maggiore]__ Terme, & found for signs to a campsite out in the country. It is big & could be dreadful but the people are friendly & we are very fortunate to be camped right on the edge with a marvellous view. __Arizona__***** With all mod cons here, we are looking out across a scene from a mediaeval book of hours. I can see little fields from here - at least 15 of them, all of different colours & textu[res] & woodland & solitary trees, old farm houses & barns, beautiful tractored rows of hay drying in the sun. The house across the way, the children have remarked, looks like the first illustration in "James & the Giant Peach".
  The camp itself has 3 swimming pools, 2 shoots, a throbbing disco (until 3am) a laundrette & a very good restaurant where
pages.notes: Text continues from a previous page (mid-sentence at top). Page number 14 visible in top-right corner. 'Salsomaggiore Terme' has the word 'Terme' written as a superscript annotation above the line. 'Arizona' is underlined and followed by asterisks — appears to be the campsite name/rating. Text cuts off at the bottom of the page mid-sentence ('where'). Some words at the right margin are partially cut off: 'marvellous', 'textu[res]', 'farm houses'. The year cannot be determined from this page alone; volume context suggests 1989–2003.
